fix: update dependencies to resolve security vulnerabilities
- Bump @daytonaio/sdk to 0.138.0 (fixes tar vulnerability)
- Bump vitest to 4.0.18 (fixes esbuild vulnerability)
- Add fast-xml-parser override to 5.3.4 (fixes DoS vulnerability)
- Update scan test syntax for vitest 4.x API

Resolves all npm audit vulnerabilities (was 28 high/moderate, now 0)

feat: fallback url when hitting cold starts (#1113)
* feat: add cold start fallback for superagent models

Add automatic fallback to always-on endpoint when primary Cloud Run
endpoints experience cold starts.

- Add timeout-based fallback (default 5s) using AbortController (TS) / asyncio (Python)
- Add DEFAULT_FALLBACK_URL constant pointing to https://superagent.sh/api/fallback
- Add getFallbackUrl() helper with priority: client option > env var > default
- Add FallbackOptions interface/dataclas

chore: bump cli and mcp to use safety-agent v0.1.4
- cli: 0.1.3 → 0.1.4
- mcp: 0.1.2 → 0.1.3
- Update safety-agent dependency to ^0.1.4

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>

feat: add cold start fallback for superagent models
Add automatic fallback to always-on endpoint when primary Cloud Run
endpoints experience cold starts.

- Add timeout-based fallback (default 5s) using AbortController (TS) / asyncio (Python)
- Add DEFAULT_FALLBACK_URL constant pointing to https://superagent.sh/api/fallback
- Add getFallbackUrl() helper with priority: client option > env var > default
- Add FallbackOptions interface/dataclass for configuration
- Add enableFallback, fallbackTimeo

Update guard API docs to include file upload and multipart support (#1063)
* Update guard API docs to include file upload and multipart support

- Added multipart/form-data content type to guard API endpoint
- Added file parameter for optional PDF file uploads
- Updated description to mention PDF file analysis support
- Added file validation error example to 400 response

This aligns the documentation with the recent SDK changes that added PDF support to the guard functionality.

feat: add redact command to CLI
- Added new `redact` command to CLI for redacting sensitive data
- Uses existing `redactSensitiveData` function from superagent-ai SDK
- Supports both command line arguments and stdin input
- Updated README with usage examples and documentation
- Closes #1045

feat: Add Redis connection pooling for proxy caching layers
Implements connection pooling for both Rust and Node.js proxy implementations
to improve performance and resource management in multitenant environments.

Rust changes:
- Add connection-manager feature to Redis dependency
- Replace single Redis client with ConnectionManager for true connection pooling
- Update all Redis operations to use connection manager
- Improve error handling and logging for connection pool

Node.js changes:
- Add

Rename vibekit-proxy to ai-proxy and rebrand as Superagent
- Renamed package from vibekit-proxy to ai-proxy
- Updated all references in codebase, workflows, and documentation
- Changed branding from VibeKit to Superagent in user-facing text
- Updated version to 0.0.1 for both Node.js and Rust packages
- Maintained vibekit.yaml config file name for compatibility
- Fixed Rust crate imports for new package name
- Updated GitHub workflows for ai-proxy artifacts
- Both Node.js and Rust implementation

Enable parallel compilation for faster CI builds
- Change codegen-units from 1 to 4 for parallel code generation
- Switch from full LTO to thin LTO for faster linking
- Add explicit --jobs 4 flag to cargo build command
- Should significantly reduce build times while maintaining performance
